title: Unknown Signal
desc: Your scanner detects something that shouldn't exist — and you're not the only one looking for it.
chainDesc: Track an unknown threat across the UnderWorld alongside Chaor himself.

steps.text:
You're deep in UnderWorld City when your scanner picks up something wrong. Not a creature, not Mugic, not any energy signature in your database. It's a frequency you've never seen before — organic, pulsing, and completely unclassifiable. It doesn't match OverWorld, UnderWorld, Mipedian, or Danian signatures. It doesn't match anything.

You're recalibrating when the ground shakes. A wall of heat slams into you like a furnace door thrown open.

Chaor steps out of the darkness. Eight feet of muscle, fire, and fury. But he's not looking at you — he's looking past you, toward the source of the signal. His eyes narrow.

Then he notices your scanner. The readings on your screen. His gaze snaps to you.

"Your device. It's picking up the signal too." It's not a question.

options.label: Try to scan Chaor while he's distracted

steps.text:
Chaor snatches your scanner out of your hands and stares at the readings. For a moment you think he's going to crush it. Instead, he shoves it back at you.

"Something has been appearing in my territory. Artifacts. Objects that shouldn't exist. They're not from any tribe I've ever seen, and I've conquered most of them." His jaw tightens. "My scouts found the first one three cycles ago. It was... growing. Into the stone. Like it was alive."

He looks at you with an expression of genuine unease poorly masked by anger.

"Your scanner can detect what my senses can't. You're coming with me to track these things down. That's not a request."

He turns and starts walking. The crowd of UnderWorld creatures parts for him like water.

"Keep up, human. The first one was spotted near Lava Pond. Magmon's been watching it."
